## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service — even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer — your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

aiTitle: Find GitHub Actions release alternative with changelog

content.text: I'll explore both the current workflow (to find where "release with changelog" is used) and the available actions library to understand your options.

input.prompt:
Explore the repository at /Users/valeriocetorelli/IdeaProjects/fhai-wrty-resource-docker, specifically the GitHub Actions workflows under .github/workflows/.

I'm looking for any usage of an action or step related to "release with changelog" — likely something referencing a "release" action, a "changelog" action, or a reusable workflow.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+'
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
#          - name: build
#            tags: build:11,build:latest
#          - name: build-base
#            tags: build-base:11,build-base:latest
          - name: python-poetry
            run_get_poetry_version: true
            platforms: linux/arm64
          - name: corretto-djl
            tag: 0.26.0
            platforms: linux/amd64

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Get POETRY_VERSION
        if: matrix.run_get_poetry_version == true
        shell: bash
        run: |
          POETRY_VERSION=$(grep "^ENV POETRY_VERSION" python-poetry/Dockerfile | cut -d' ' -f3)
          echo -n "DOCKER_TAGS=421213061221.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/python-poetry:3.11-${POETRY_VERSION}-arm64,421213061221.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/python-poetry:latest" > $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Create DOCKER_TAGS
        if: matrix.run_get_poetry_version == false
        shell: bash
        run: echo -n "DOCKER_TAGS=421213061221.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/${{ matrix.name }}:${{ matrix.tag }},421213061221.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/${{ matrix.name }}:latest" > $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Set up QEMU
        uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v3

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4.0.1
        with:
          aws-region: eu-west-1
          role-session-name: gha-wrty-ecr-push
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::738822569749:role/GitHubHostedRunnerOIDC

      - name: Login to Amazon ECR
        id: login-ecr
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2
        with:
          registries: '421213061221'

      - run: echo -n " I should push ${{  startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/master') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags') }}  on ${{ github.ref }}"

      - name: Build Docker image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ./${{ matrix.name }}
          file: ./${{ matrix.name }}/Dockerfile
          platforms: ${{ matrix.platforms }}
          provenance: false
          push: ${{  startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/master') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags') }}
          tags: ${{ env.DOCKER_TAGS }}

  Slack:
    uses: meltwater/wrapidity-github-actions/.github/workflows/send-slack.yml@master
    needs: build
    with:
      project_name: docker-images
    secrets: inherit


  Release:
    needs: build
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    uses: meltwater/wrapidity-github-actions/.github/workflows/release-with-changelog.yml@master
    secrets: inherit

name: Release (auto tag + GitHub Release)

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      default_bump:
        description: 'Semver bump: #major / #minor / #patch / #none'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'minor'
      java_version:
        description: 'JDK version used to run mvn versions:set'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '17'
      java_distribution:
        description: 'JDK distribution'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'corretto'
    outputs:
      version:
        description: 'The new tag (e.g. v4.4.0)'
        value: ${{ jobs.release.outputs.version }}

jobs:
  release:
    if: inputs.default_bump != 'none'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_tag }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.PAT || github.token }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Java
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: ${{ inputs.java_distribution }}
          java-version: ${{ inputs.java_version }}
          cache: 'maven'

      - name: Generate settings.xml for Maven
        uses: whelk-io/maven-settings-xml-action@v22
        env:
          ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME }}
          ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD }}
        with:
          repositories: >
            [
              {
                "id": "wrapidity-snapshots",
                "name": "Wrapidity Snapshots",
                "url": "https://meltwater.jfrog.io/meltwater/wrapidity/",
                "releases": { "enabled": "false", "updatePolicy": "always", "checksumPolicy": "fail" },
                "snapshots": { "enabled": "true",  "updatePolicy": "never",  "checksumPolicy": "fail" }
              },
              {
                "id": "wrapidity-releases",
                "name": "Wrapidity Releases",
                "url": "https://meltwater.jfrog.io/meltwater/wrapidity/",
                "releases": { "enabled": "true",  "updatePolicy": "always", "checksumPolicy": "fail" },
                "snapshots": { "enabled": "false", "updatePolicy": "never",  "checksumPolicy": "fail" }
              }
            ]
          servers: >
            [
              { "id": "wrapidity-releases",  "username": "${env.ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME}", "password": "${env.ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD}" },
              { "id": "wrapidity-snapshots", "username": "${env.ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME}", "password": "${env.ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD}" }
            ]

      - name: Determine next version
        id: version
        uses: anothrNick/github-tag-action@f278d49d30cdd8775cc3e7dd00b5ee11686ee297 # 1.71.0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT || github.token }}
          DEFAULT_BUMP: ${{ inputs.default_bump }}
          WITH_V: true
          DRY_RUN: true

      - name: Bump pom and push release tag
        env:
          TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_tag }}
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT || github.token }}
          ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME }}
          ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          NEW_VERSION="${TAG#v}"
          git config user.name  "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          mvn -B versions:set -DnewVersion="$NEW_VERSION" -DgenerateBackupPoms=false
          git commit -am "Release $TAG"
          git tag -a "$TAG" -m "Release $TAG"
          git push origin "$TAG"

      - name: Generate changelog
        id: changelog
        uses: requarks/changelog-action@b78a3354a01f4a1affb484b9264b506a815c46b1 # v1.10.3
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.PAT || github.token }}
          tag: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_tag }}
          writeToFile: false

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: ncipollo/release-action@339a81892b84b4eeb0f6e744e4574d79d0d9b8dd # v1.21.0
        with:
          tag: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_tag }}
          name: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_tag }}
          body: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.changes }}
          token: ${{ secrets.PAT || github.token }}
          allowUpdates: true

input.content:
# Fix broken Release job in build-images.yml

## Context

`build-images.yml` in this repo (`fhai-wrty-resource-docker`) has a `Release` job that calls a
reusable workflow which **no longer exists**:

```yaml
Release:
  needs: build
  if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
  uses: meltwater/wrapidity-github-actions/.github/workflows/release-with-changelog.yml@master
  secrets: inherit
```

That file was **deleted** in `wrapidity-github-actions` commit `b3b8b99` (2026-06-02) and replaced
with `release-auto-tag.yml`. So the reference fails to resolve and the `Release` job breaks.

`release-auto-tag.yml` is **not** a valid replacement here because it:
- runs `mvn versions:set` — but this is a Docker-images repo with **no `pom.xml`**,
- needs Artifactory/PAT secrets not used anywhere in this repo's `.github/`,
- **creates the tag itself** (auto-bump) — but `build-images.yml` is already *triggered by* a pushed
  semver tag (`on: push: tags: v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+`) and the job is gated on
  `if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')`, so the tag already exists.

**Intended outcome:** when a `vX.Y.Z` tag is pushed, create a GitHub Release **from that existing
tag** — no tagging, no Maven. Self-contained in this repo, using GitHub's auto-generated notes
(the behavior the old `release-with-changelog.yml`, which just called `softprops/action-gh-release`,
actually had).

## Change

File: `.github/workflows/build-images.yml` (lines 86–90)

Replace the broken reusable-workflow call with an inline job:

```yaml
  Release:
    needs: build
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          generate_release_notes: true
```

Notes:
- No `secrets: inherit` needed — the step uses the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
- `contents: write` is already granted at the top of the file (line 12), but declaring it on the
  job keeps the job self-documenting; it is harmless to also keep the top-level grant.
- `github.ref_name` on a tag push is the bare tag (e.g. `v1.2.3`).
- `generate_release_notes: true` = GitHub auto-generates notes from PRs/commits since the last tag.
- Bumped the action from the old `@v0.1.15` to `@v2` (current major).

## Verification

1. **Static check** — parse/lint the workflow before pushing:
   - `actionlint .github/workflows/build-images.yml` (if installed), or a YAML syntax check.
2. **End-to-end (requires pushing a tag — outward action, confirm first):**
   - Push a semver tag, e.g. `git tag v0.0.1-test && git push origin v0.0.1-test`.
   - Watch the run: `gh run watch` (or the Actions tab). Confirm `build` → `Release` succeed and a
     new GitHub Release appears for that tag with auto-generated notes:
     `gh release view v0.0.1-test`.
   - Clean up the throwaway release/tag afterward:
     `gh release delete v0.0.1-test --cleanup-tag` (or delete the tag manually).
